title: CanonicalBehaviorEvent
description: One decision unit: an actor (or population) about to produce an observed behavior at a cutoff, together with everything known BEFORE that behavior. The task-specific label lives in `payload.target`; nothing in the record above `payload.target` may reveal the future. This is a thin outer envelope shared by every dataset + a task-specific `payload` validated separately against schemas/task_payloads/<task_type>.schema.json.

record_id.description: Globally unique, deterministic id: '<dataset_id>:<task_type>:<stable-hash>'. Reproducible from raw source + converter version.

task_type.description: One of registry/task_taxonomy.yaml. Determines which payload schema applies.

license_class.description: Coarse class from registry/licenses.yaml (e.g. permissive_commercial, cc_by, research_only, restricted).

episode_id.description: Stable id for the interaction/session/trial-sequence this decision belongs to. The primary leakage-isolation candidate.

sequence_index.description: Position of THIS decision unit within its episode (0-based).

cutoff_time.description: Everything in context/known_history must be <= this time.

cutoff_sequence_index.description: Everything in context must be strictly before this index within the episode.

future_hidden.description: MUST be true. A record with future_hidden=false is a leakage bug, not valid training data.

population_id.description: Set for population/aggregate tasks instead of a single actor.

persistent_identity_available.description: Whether actor_id is stable across episodes (enables cross-scenario history).

actor_profile.description: Stable, pre-cutoff attributes of the actor/population (demographics, persona, personality). Open object.

private_state_before.description: Actor's private state KNOWN to have existed before the cutoff (e.g. self-reported goal, private value). Only include if the source actually recorded it.

known_history.description: Ordered list of prior observable events (messages, actions, choices) strictly before the cutoff.

current_observation.description: What the actor observes at the cutoff (the latest message, the current screen, the offered item).

world_state.description: Institutional/environmental state at cutoff (listing details, game board, rules).

available_actions.description: The recoverable action space at the cutoff, or null when unknown. null != empty: null means 'we do not know the choice set'.

payload.description: Task-specific input+target. Validated separately against schemas/task_payloads/<task_type>.schema.json. `payload.target` is the label; nothing outside `payload.target` may reveal it.

causal_metadata.description: Only populated for interventional/experimental datasets. Empty object otherwise.

assignment_mechanism.description: e.g. rct, bernoulli_ts, random, uniform_random_exposure, observational, unknown

propensity.description: Logging-policy probability of the taken action, when known (bandit datasets).

missing_fields.description: Fields the source did not provide (stored null/empty, never fabricated).

inferred_fields.description: Fields deterministically derived (NOT model-generated). e.g. sequence_index computed from ordering.

weak_label_fields.description: Fields that are model-generated annotations. MUST be empty in default training manifests.

code_commit.description: Git commit of the converter code at conversion time.

transformation_steps.description: Ordered human-readable list of transformations applied to the raw record.

raw_record_locator.description: Exact pointer(s) back to the raw source record(s) this example derives from.

content_hash.description: Hash of the canonical record content (dedup + integrity).

split_metadata.description: Populated by the splitting stage; null/empty before splitting.

isolation_keys.description: The concrete key values that decided this record's split (e.g. {participant: p12, topic: abortion}).
